notes: Pass rate delta is +83 percentage points: 22/22 with skill vs 4/22 without skill. Skill cleanly differentiates on every test case.
Strong differentiator on preventive evals (eval-0, eval-1) — baseline scored 0/5 on both. The pitfalls the catalog targets are exactly the LLM-default-trap shape — baseline reliably falls into them.

Eval-2 (retrospective git script) is the weakest differentiator: baseline got 3/6 because the technical answer (reorder ops) is reachable from general git knowledge. The skill still wins because it generalizes the pattern beyond this script and explicitly rejects --autostash with reasoning.

Eval-3 (retrospective qbit netns) shows the catalog's biggest value: the baseline confidently produced the WRONG mechanism (gluetun tunnel stall) while the skill produced the right one (parent-restart-orphans-child). Wrong-but-confident is the most dangerous failure mode for a homelab assistant.
Token cost: with-skill uses ~52% more tokens than baseline (25k vs 16.5k mean). Expected — the skill loads pitfall files and PM templates progressively. Justified by the pass-rate delta.
Time cost: with-skill is ~37% slower (62s vs 45s mean), driven mostly by file reads. Acceptable for the quality lift.
